title: [lora_l2.c:893] lichen_lora_l2_tx len cast overflow for size_t > UINT32_MAX
description:
Contract: lichen_lora_l2_tx receives size_t len parameter
Violation: On line 988, len is cast to uint32_t for lora_send(). While the code validates len <= 255, on 64-bit platforms where size_t > 32 bits, if len validation were ever bypassed or changed, the cast could silently truncate.
Impact: Currently low (len is capped at 255), but the cast relies on implicit contract that LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD fits in uint32_t. A compile-time assertion would make this explicit.
Mitigation: Add BUILD_ASSERT(LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD <= UINT32_MAX)
close_reason: Added BUILD_ASSERT(sizeof(size_t) <= sizeof(uint32_t)) at the lora_send() cast site. This guards against compilation on 64-bit platforms where size_t could exceed uint32_t. On target 32-bit MCUs (nRF52/STM32/ESP32), size_t == uint32_t so the assertion passes. Expanded comment to document both the runtime safety (len bounded to 255) and the platform assumption.
